markers.finding: Low acceleration on straight: 0.44g (expected: >0.70g for 943m straight at 129 km/h entry).
markers.reasoning: The car achieved only 0.44g maximum acceleration, which is below expected performance for this straight. For a 943m segment entered at 129 km/h, expect at least 0.70g acceleration.
markers.action: Apply throttle more aggressively on straights. If wheelspin occurs, reduce throttle slightly but maintain high acceleration. Check for traction issues or mechanical problems if acceleration remains low.

markers.finding: 5 wheel lockup event(s) in braking zone.
markers.reasoning: A locked wheel generates less braking force than a wheel at the slip limit (~10% slip). Each lockup extends the braking distance by 2-5 meters and flat-spots the tire, reducing grip for the rest of the session.
markers.action: Reduce initial brake pressure by 5-10% and apply the brake more progressively. The first 50ms of brake application should not be at maximum pressure. Squeeze the brake — do not stab it.

markers.finding: 7 wheel lockup event(s) outside corner braking zones.
markers.reasoning: Lockups outside of braking zones may indicate ABS issues, debris on track, or mid-corner instability.
markers.action: Review brake application technique on entry to straights. If ABS is not intervening, check brake bias.

markers.finding: 0.24s of coasting between brake release and throttle application.
markers.reasoning: During 0.24s of coast, the car travels ~1m with no acceleration or deceleration. This is pure time loss to aerodynamic drag.
markers.action: Overlap brake release with throttle application. Target coast time < 0.05s. As the left foot releases the brake, the right foot should already be progressively applying throttle.

markers.finding: Brake released fully before steering input. No trail-braking detected.
markers.reasoning: Releasing the brake before turning unloads the front axle at exactly the moment you need front grip for turn-in. This forces a slower entry speed or causes understeer. Trail-braking keeps weight forward, enabling higher entry speed and a tighter line.
markers.action: Maintain light brake pressure (20-30% of peak) as you begin steering. Progressively release the brake as steering angle increases. Target: brake pressure should reach zero at or just past the apex.

markers.finding: Only 0.3% of straight time at full throttle (expected: >100.0% for 943m straight at 129 km/h entry).
markers.reasoning: The car spent 99.7% of the straight with partial throttle, missing out on maximum acceleration. For a 943m straight entered at 129 km/h, expect at least 100.0% full throttle time.
markers.action: Apply full throttle immediately upon entering the straight and maintain it until the next corner. Only lift off throttle for gear changes or if wheelspin occurs.

markers.finding: Rear wheelspin detected on corner exit.
markers.reasoning: The rear tires exceeded their traction limit during acceleration. Spinning tires generate less forward force than rolling tires, so the car actually accelerates slower despite more throttle input. It also risks snap oversteer.
markers.action: Apply throttle more progressively on exit. Wait until the steering is unwinding before increasing throttle past 50%. Think: 'unwind the wheel, then add throttle' — not both at once.
